sections.paragraph:
ἕτερος, α, ον [IE; ‘one of two’] distributive pronoun

—1. as marker numerically distinguishing one item from another other, another: of two Mt 6:24; Lk 5:7; 9:56; 18:10; Ac 23:6; 1 Cor 4:6; of more than two Mt 8:21; 11:3; Lk 6:6; 22:58 (ἕ. = someone else: a man); J 19:37; Ro 8:39; 13:9; 1 Cor 3:4; 12:9f; 1 Ti 1:10—cp. the use of the pl. Lk 3:18; 10:1; 11:26; Eph 3:5; Phil 2:4; 2 Ti 2:2.

—2. as marker generically or qualitatively expressing dissimilarity of one item relative to another item other, another, different Mk 16:12; Lk 9:29; Ac 2:4; Ro 7:3f; Gal 1:6 (cp. ἑτεροδιδασκαλέω); 1 Cor 15:40; Js 2:25; Jd 7.
